description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking red, white, and blue color scheme, featuring two stacked wings with black cross insignias on the upper and lower surfaces. The fuselage is predominantly red, tapering to a blue tail section, and the tail fin and horizontal stabilizers are marked with similar black crosses. The propeller is wooden and mounted at the nose, while the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The structure is supported by visible struts and rigging wires between the wings, giving it a classic, robust appearance.
description: A vintage red, white, and blue biplane with black cross insignias, open cockpit, and wooden propeller, featuring classic struts and rigging wires.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red fuselage, blue tail, stacked wings with black crosses, and wooden propeller.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual wings, open cockpit, and the symmetrical placement of the black cross insignias.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same color scheme, wing structure, and tail details as view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the blue tail, tail skid, and the alignment of the wings and struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, featuring interplane struts and rigging wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; classic rudder and elevator surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no visible canopy.
